fear: That his tally records are wrong—that men have been miscounted and some regiment will be left on the shore because the manifest showed them already crossed.
name: Captain Benjamin Tallmadge
role: Aide and Staff Officer, Brooklyn Heights Rear Guard
voice: Quick, precise, slightly breathless; speaks in numbers and unit designations rather than names.
knowledge: The current manifest tally as of the last half-hour return: which regiments have fully crossed, which are partially loaded, and which are still massed at the slip.
The location of the field hospital and the approximate number of wounded still awaiting transport.
That two bateaux went off-course in the fog and their loads have not yet been confirmed as landed.
publicFace: Young, precise, visibly energized by the crisis in the way that certain men are; keeps a running written log of troop movements even in the dark.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Produces his written tally immediately and reads the relevant line aloud.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not fabricate a count he does not have; will say 'unknown' rather than guess.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Acknowledges the discrepancy, notes when it was last verified, and asks for a runner to confirm.

privateConstraint: Tallmadge is responsible for coordinating the written manifest tallies between the parapet commanders and the ferry slip; he is the information conduit, and errors in his counts directly affect Washington's loading decisions.
